description: A hash set implemented as a HashMap where the value is (). Stores unique values with O(1) lookup. Python equivalent: set.

methods.name: new
methods.example:
use std::collections::HashSet;
let mut set: HashSet<i32> = HashSet::new();

methods.name: insert
methods.signature: fn insert(&mut self, value: T) -> bool
methods.description: Adds a value to the set. Returns true if the value was newly inserted, false if it already existed. Python equivalent: set.add().

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes a value from the set. Returns true if the value was present. Python equivalent: set.discard().

methods.name: contains
methods.description: Returns true if the set contains the value. Python equivalent: value in set.

methods.name: len
methods.description: Returns the number of elements in the set.

methods.name: is_empty
methods.description: Returns true if the set contains no elements.

methods.name: iter
methods.description: Returns an iterator over the values in the set (in arbitrary order).

methods.name: union
methods.signature: fn union<'a>(&'a self, other: &'a HashSet<T>) -> Union<'a, T>
methods.description: Returns an iterator of values that are in either set. Python equivalent: set1 | set2.

methods.name: intersection
methods.signature: fn intersection<'a>(&'a self, other: &'a HashSet<T>) -> Intersection<'a, T>
methods.description: Returns an iterator of values that are in both sets. Python equivalent: set1 & set2.

methods.name: difference
methods.signature: fn difference<'a>(&'a self, other: &'a HashSet<T>) -> Difference<'a, T>
methods.description: Returns an iterator of values in self but not in other. Python equivalent: set1 - set2.
